---
title: "Yee Kwan Wong"
---

<style type="text/css">
.title {
  display: none;
}
img {
  border-radius: 200px 200px 200px 100px;
}

</style>

<div class="row" style="text-align: center; padding-top: 50px; padding-right: 20px;padding-left: 20px">

# Yee Kwan Wong

`yee.kwan.wong@emory.edu`

<img src="img/ykw_img.jpg" alt="photo" width="200" height="200" style="float:center">

</div>

<div class="row" style="text-align: justify; text-justify: inter-word;  padding-top: 2px;padding-right: 20px;padding-left: 20px; font-size: 18px">

## Introduction

I am Assistant Professor of Chinese at Oxford College of Emory University. I hold a PhD in East Asian Studies from the University of California, Irvine. I am originally from Hong Kong, and my research interests include modern Chinese literature and film, the intersection between philosophy and literature, and mixed-race studies. Currently, I am preparing my first monograph, tentatively titled "Mixed Messages: Racial and Ethnic Ambiguities in Modern Chinese Literature." 



### Recent publications:

[An Untranslatable Hong Kong story: Wong Bik-wan’s “Nausea”](https://writingchinesejournal.org/articles/10.22599/wcj.46)

["The Poetics of Water in Dung Kai-cheung’s (Meta-)Apocalyptic Tale," in the forthcoming edited volume *Entangled Waterscapes in Asia*](https://brill.com/display/title/70628)

### Translations:

[Wong Bik-wan's "Nausea," in *Cha: an Asian Literary Journal*](https://chajournal.blog/2024/04/14/nausea/)

[Lai Hsiang-yin's "Island," in *Taiwan Literature: English Translation Series, No. 50*](https://press.ntu.edu.tw/en/publish/show.php?act=book&refer=ntup_book01313)

### Media appearance (in Chinese):

[电影36：《破·地狱》(The Last Dance)|死者何以死，生者何以生？](https://www.xiaoyuzhoufm.com/episode/6a333245bc49fb95db0db8d8)

</div>
